meaning: n.涓嶈兘璐┛,娣卞ゥ,涓嶅彲娴嬬煡
examples: So matter has the property of impenetrability.
The complexity of the society originates from the contingency, ambiguousness and impenetrability of human consciousness and from the "double contingency"in the communication.
Banhart has achieved cult status on many college radio stations, attracting listeners who find the impenetrability of his lyrics to be part of his appeal.
Yet for all their seeming impenetrability they were flexible, thanks to an ingenious lamellar structure essentially, strips made of lacquered metal bound together with colored or gold laces.
